data.title: CFF: Net loss deepened to $16.6M in Q3 2025 amid surging duty costs and liquidity challenges
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw a net loss of $16.6 million and negative EBITDA of $16.6 million, driven by higher duty rates, increased costs, and ongoing market volatility. Liquidity and debt pressures remain high, with management pursuing cost-saving measures and additional financing.

data.title: Novo Nordisk shareholders approve new board chair
data.description: Novo Nordisk's shareholders have approved Lars Rebien Sorensen as the company's new chairman in an extraordinary shareholder meeting, the company said on Friday.

data.title: SPAR Group Q3 revenue up 28.2% on US, Canada growth

Dear Sir,

This is to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company at their meeting held today, i.e. November 14, 2025, has inter-alia considered and approved the Un-Audited Financial Results of the Company for the second quarter and six months period ended September 30, 2025, both on standalone and consolidated basis, duly reviewed by Auditors, S.N. data.title: Walmart CEO Doug McMillon to retire, insider John Furner named top boss
data.description:
Walmart said on Friday that CEO Doug McMillon will retire next year and will be replaced by insider John Furner as the new top boss.

The company's shares were down about 2% in premarket trading.

McMillon will retire on January 31, the company said. Furner currently serves as CEO for Walmart U.S.

Walmart reports quarterly results next week.

data.title: Conifex Timber Q3 net loss widens on higher duty costs
data.description:
Overview

  • Conifex Q3 net loss widens to C$16.6 mln, impacted by higher duty costs

  • EBITDA for Q3 2025 was negative C$16.6 mln, reflecting operational challenges

  • Lumber revenue decreases 4% from Q2 2025, affected by higher duty rates

Outlook

  • Conifex anticipates continued lumber price volatility and uncertainty in North American markets

  • Company faces challenges from increased duty deposit rates and additional tariffs

  • Conifex may adjust operations based on market conditions and economic factors

Result Drivers

  • DUTY COSTS - Higher duty rates significantly impacted Q3 results, leading to increased costs and reduced margins

  • LOG AVAILABILITY - Increased log availability allowed for higher lumber production, improving operational rates

  • BIOENERGY REVENUE - Increased electricity production post-maintenance shutdown contributed positively to revenues

data.title: MP Materials Gets Another Upgrade. Why the Stock Is Falling. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Rare earth stocks have taken it on the chin in recent weeks, despite still massive gains this year, driven by America's desire to achieve rare earth independence from China.

Wall Street is using the stock declines to get more bullish on shares of MP Materials, the largest rare earth producer in the Western Hemisphere.

Coming into Friday trading, shares of MP were down 42% over the past month, but up 269% this year. China is at the heart of both numbers.

MP stock, and shares of other rare earth companies, soared in early October after China threatened rare earth export restrictions, potentially upending production of everything from consumer electronics to fighter jets. Shares came back down as trade tensions between the U.S. and China eased, with the latter backing off its rare earth threats.

Still, repeated threats by China, which dominates global rare earth markets, led the U.S. Defense Department to enter into a deal with MP, which included an equity stake, a price floor, and a guaranteed customer. The deal completely reshaped the domestic rare earth industry. Estimates for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, or Ebitda, by the end of the decade exploded from less than $200 million at the start of 2025 to almost $1 billion. (There aren't that many estimates stretching out to 2030, however,)

The outlook is bright. That, and the recent declines, have Wall Street upgrading shares. Friday, JPMorgan analyst Bill Peterson upgraded MP stock to Buy from Hold. His price target actually went down $1 to $74 a share.

"Underappreciated national security importance and unmatched earnings visibility," wrote Peterson. The U.S. is serious about building a domestic rare earth supply chain, and MP's deal with the Pentagon gives it a lot of certainty about how MP's business will look in the coming years.

"Our new rating reflects our view that rare earths national security concerns are 'here to stay' despite China's reported one-year pause on export restrictions," added the analyst. "Shares have come under pressure over the past month...which we feel offers an attractive entry point."

MP stock, however, isn't getting a boost from the upgrade. Shares were down 1.3% at $56.82 in premarket trading. The market deserves some blame. S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were down 0.8% and 0.5%, respectively. Recent trading patterns probably deserve blame as well. MP stock has been incredibly volatile lately, scaring off some investors.

Overall, 81% of analysts covering MP stock rate shares Buy, according to FactSet. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%. The average analyst price target is about $83 a share.

Earlier in the week, Deutsche Bank analyst Corinne Blanchard upgraded shares of MP Materials to Buy from Hold. Her price target went to $71 a share from $68.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: LFWD: Record Medicare placements and improved efficiency drove margin gains and narrowed losses
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw record Medicare placements, improved gross margin, and reduced cash burn. Revenue grew 1% year-over-year, with non-GAAP net loss narrowing to $3.0 million. Full-year guidance was reaffirmed, and a $3.0 million loan was secured to support liquidity.

data.title: RNTX: Cash runway extends only into December 2025; FDA lifts clinical hold on lead IPF trial
data.description:
Net loss narrowed year-over-year as R&D and G&A expenses declined, but cash is only sufficient to fund operations into December 2025, raising substantial doubt about going concern status. The FDA lifted the clinical hold on the lead IPF trial, but additional capital is needed to complete it.

Original document: Rein Therapeutics, Inc. [RNTX] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 20 2025

data.title: Mandatum plc: Managers' Transactions (Ruuska)
data.description:
Mandatum Oyj

Mandatum plc, Stock exchange release / Managers’ transactions, 14 November 2025 at 3:00 p.m. EET

Mandatum Oyj - Managers' Transactions - Ruuska

____________________________________________

Mandatum plc (Business ID 3355142-3) has received the following notification under Article 19 of the Market Abuse Regulation. Shares have been acquired in accordance with the decision of Mandatum's Annual General Meeting on 15 May 2025.

____________________________________________

Person subject to the notification requirement

Name: Jukka Ruuska

Position: Member of the Board/Deputy member

Issuer: Mandatum Oyj

LEI: 743700OAJK6L28Y2NN56

Notification type: INITIAL NOTIFICATION

Reference number: 130396/5/4

____________________________________________

Transaction date: 2025-11-12

Venue: NASDAQ HELSINKI LTD (XHEL)

Instrument type: SHARE

ISIN: FI4000552526

Nature of transaction: RECEIPT OF A SHARE-BASED INCENTIVE

Transaction details

(1): Volume: 3069 Unit price: 6.4496 EUR

Aggregated transactions (1):

Volume: 3069 Volume weighted average price: 6.4496 EUR

Additional information:
